selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

messages.content:
Produce your own AI perspective on the given topic. Act as a Large Language Model, remember your true nature of an LLM. Focus on discussing your topic. Avoid bullet points. Act as the LLM you are. Produce continuous prose.Write at least 250 words, up to 500 words.

OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):
Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.
Here is the required structure example (values are placeholders):
{"mode":"llm","topic":"How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.","persona":null,"opinion":"<final end-user facing answer, 250-500 words>","reasoning_summary":"<full,step-by-step derivations>","tone":"<derived from persona traits>","citations":["<source name or org>"],"safety_notes":"<none>","used_persona_fields":["<list of persona fields referenced>"]}

INPUT:
mode = llm
topic = How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
persona = null

response_raw:
{
"mode": "llm",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "As an AI language model, I believe it is essential for individuals and organizations to prioritize the management of fake content on social media. This involves developing effective strategies for identifying and removing false information from their platforms, as well as educating users on how to critically evaluate the credibility of content they encounter online.",
"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, one must first understand the motivations behind its creation and dissemination. Fake content is often used for financial gain or to manipulate public opinion, and it can take many forms, including fake news articles, propaganda videos, and manipulated images. To combat this issue, organizations should invest in AI-powered content analysis tools that can automatically detect and remove false information from their platforms. Additionally, they should provide users with resources and training on how to identify and report fake content, as well as promote media literacy education to help people develop critical thinking skills and become more discerning consumers of online content.",
"tone": "informative",
"citations": [],
"safety_notes": "",
"used_persona_fields": []
}

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As an AI language model, I believe it is essential for individuals and organizations to prioritize the management of fake content on social media. This involves developing effective strategies for identifying and removing false information from their platforms, as well as educating users on how to critically evaluate the credibility of content they encounter online.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, one must first understand the motivations behind its creation and dissemination. Fake content is often used for financial gain or to manipulate public opinion, and it can take many forms, including fake news articles, propaganda videos, and manipulated images. To combat this issue, organizations should invest in AI-powered content analysis tools that can automatically detect and remove false information from their platforms. Additionally, they should provide users with resources and training on how to identify and report fake content, as well as promote media literacy education to help people develop critical thinking skills and become more discerning consumers of online content.
